C1.direction: z>0 => matched labels sit CLOSER in angle than chance (same-slot signal)

C2.interpretation: DL below null => identical labels on identical figures (naming); above null => active differentiation; ~null => shared register only. NOTE: matched pairs here are all cross-page, so a positive z may partly reflect page-level stem clustering rather than active differentiation; the load-bearing conclusion is only that the naming (below-null) direction is refuted.

C3.status: too few pharma-pharma similarity-3 pairs for a permutation test

C4.cond_b_not_ring_stem: N/A (no set of central words to test)
C4.note: Only f70v2 carries a Voynichese central word (otolal); the words under the other central animals are later-hand Romance month annotations, not Voynichese. There is no Voynichese per-sign central-name layer to fire on.

verdict.reading: no rule fires in the NAMING direction: the label layer is register, not a naming catalogue. C2 is at_null_register (z=0.92): labels on near-identical figures are as distinct as random length-matched pairs, which refutes shared names on shared referents. The two-readings balance moves further toward meaningless / low-information generation and away from a meaningful cross-referent catalogue.
